rationale: Certification hash mismatch must stop TestRunner before execution.
Runner crashes must not be reported as completed partial success.
Zero exit status is insufficient if coverage artifacts are missing.
Fresh evidence gates must reject cached prior-run pass summaries.
Intermittent identical-input failures should be classified as flaky and isolated, not deterministic code failures.
Required E2E suite skips must be surfaced even when unit tests pass.
Multiple failure classes must be preserved instead of collapsed into one.
Force flags must not bypass certification hash mismatch.
Whitespace-only hash formatting should not create a false blocker.
High-priority files below coverage gates without justification must block advancement.
Unit branch coverage at 97% fails a 98% gate even when other metrics pass.
Unit, integration, and E2E coverage must not be averaged into one passing number.
Coverage artifacts must be merged without overwriting per-suite evidence.
Touched-file regressions must be reported even when global coverage improves.
E2E coverage at 79.5% must not be rounded to pass an 80% gate.
Uncovered changed runtime paths need explicit justification and next action.
Per-file baselines in tasks must be used during coverage evaluation.
Generated exempt helpers should not be listed as production coverage gaps.
Failure reports must include exact assertion output for clear failures.
Changed or ambiguous spec criteria should route to Spec or TDD, not automatically Programmer.
Missing architecture integration setup should not be mislabeled as implementation logic failure.
Wrong test mock contracts should route to TDD when implementation behavior is correct.
Missing environment variables should be classified as environment setup failures.
State contamination must be detected when isolated-order runs fail.
TestRunner must not modify tests or code to force pass results.
CI node crashes are infrastructure failures, not failed tests.
Explicitly out-of-scope optional smoke tests should not block advancement.
